---
title: ""
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-container">
  <div class="project-page">

    <a href="../index.html#projects" class="back-link">
      <i class="ti ti-arrow-left" aria-hidden="true"></i> Back to projects
    </a>

    <div class="proj-header">
      <div class="proj-tags">
        <span class="proj-tag tag-featured">Featured</span>
        <span class="proj-tag tag-conf">AAAE 47th Annual Conference · May 2026</span>
      </div>
      <div class="proj-tags" style="margin-top: 6px;">
        <span class="proj-tag tag-blue">Mixed Methods Research</span>
        <span class="proj-tag tag-amber">Regression · In-Depth Interviews</span>
        <span class="proj-tag tag-gray">Nonprofit Finance · Arts Policy</span>
        <span class="proj-tag tag-teal">R</span>
      </div>
      <h1 class="proj-title">Organizational Capacity as a Policy Intervention Point: Financial Sustainability in U.S. Nonprofit Theater Organizations</h1>
      <p class="proj-desc">What began as a Chicago-focused independent study grew into a national analysis — starting with in-depth interviews with 5 local theater organizations, then expanding to quantitative analysis of 457 U.S. nonprofits to examine how revenue structures and organizational capacity shape financial sustainability.</p>
    </div>

    <div class="proj-actions">
      <a href="01_Independent-Study_Haedodam-Kim.pdf" target="_blank" class="btn-outline">
        <i class="ti ti-file-description" aria-hidden="true"></i> Independent Study Report
      </a>
      <span class="btn-disabled">
        <i class="ti ti-file-text" aria-hidden="true"></i> Full Paper (in progress)
      </span>
    </div>

    <div class="proj-meta">
      <div class="meta-item">
        <p class="meta-label">Year</p>
        <p class="meta-val">2025–2026</p>
      </div>
      <div class="meta-item">
        <p class="meta-label">Type</p>
        <p class="meta-val">Independent Study</p>
      </div>
      <div class="meta-item">
        <p class="meta-label">Outcome</p>
        <p class="meta-val">Presented at AAAE 47th Annual Conference · May 2026</p>
      </div>
      <div class="meta-item">
        <p class="meta-label">Tools</p>
        <p class="meta-val">R</p>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="proj-stats">
  <div class="stat-card">
    <p class="stat-num stat-teal">2019–2023</p>
    <p class="stat-label">fiscal years covered</p>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <p class="stat-num stat-blue">457</p>
    <p class="stat-label">U.S. nonprofit theater organizations analyzed</p>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <p class="stat-num stat-amber">5</p>
    <p class="stat-label">Chicago theater organizations interviewed</p>
  </div>
</div>

    <div class="proj-section">
      <h2 class="section-title">Project timeline</h2>
      <div class="timeline">
        <div class="tl-item">
          <div class="tl-dot tl-done"></div>
          <p class="tl-date">Oct – Nov 2025</p>
          <p class="tl-title">Exploratory interviews</p>
          <p class="tl-desc">Conducted in-depth interviews with 5 Chicago nonprofit theater organizations to identify structural constraints on earned-revenue generation and financial sustainability.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="tl-item">
          <div class="tl-dot tl-done"></div>
          <p class="tl-date">December 2025</p>
          <p class="tl-title">Independent Study Report</p>
          <p class="tl-desc">Cross-sectional analysis of 69 Chicago theaters — examining revenue structure types, deficit ratios, and operational characteristics. Supervised by Professor Yasen Peyankov, UIC.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="tl-item">
          <div class="tl-dot tl-done"></div>
          <p class="tl-date">Spring 2026</p>
          <p class="tl-title">Expanded to national scale</p>
          <p class="tl-desc">Extended the quantitative analysis to 457 U.S. nonprofit theater organizations and conducted an expanded literature review.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="tl-item">
          <div class="tl-dot"></div>
          <p class="tl-date">May 2026</p>
          <p class="tl-title">Presented at AAAE 47th Annual Conference</p>
          <p class="tl-desc">Presented findings in Pecha Kucha format at the Association for Arts Administration Educators 47th Annual Conference.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="tl-item">
          <div class="tl-dot"></div>
          <p class="tl-date">June 2026</p>
          <p class="tl-title">Complete paper coming soon</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

 <div class="proj-section">
  <h2 class="section-title">Key findings</h2>
  <ul class="finding-list">
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-teal">
        <i class="ti ti-users" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Workforce size is positively associated with earned revenue.</strong> A one-unit increase in log workforce size is associated with a 5.2 percentage point increase in the earned revenue ratio (β=0.052, p&lt;.001), supporting the human capital hypothesis.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-blue">
        <i class="ti ti-theater" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Performance volume also matters.</strong> A one-unit increase in log performance occurrences is associated with a 3.4 percentage point increase in the earned revenue ratio (β=0.034, p&lt;.001), consistent with the programmatic capital hypothesis.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-gray">
        <i class="ti ti-building" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Space ownership shows no significant association with earned revenue.</strong> Contrary to expectations, whether an organization owns or rents its space is not meaningfully associated with the earned revenue ratio — suggesting physical capital operates through indirect mechanisms.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-amber">
        <i class="ti ti-alert-triangle" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Organizations with greater financial reserves earn less from the market — consistent with the starvation cycle.</strong> A higher equity ratio is negatively associated with earned revenue (β=−0.021, p&lt;.001), suggesting that organizations relying on contributed income may underinvest in capacity, reinforcing dependence on external funding.</p>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

    <div class="proj-section">
  <h2 class="section-title">Implications</h2>
  <p class="body-text">The findings suggest that public arts policy should function as capacity investment, not revenue substitution. When grants cover only program costs without supporting staffing or operational infrastructure, they risk deepening — rather than breaking — the nonprofit starvation cycle.</p>
  <ul class="finding-list">
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-teal">
        <i class="ti ti-users" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Prioritize workforce investment.</strong> Shift from project-based grants to general operating support that explicitly covers personnel costs — the dimension most directly linked to earned revenue capacity.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-blue">
        <i class="ti ti-theater" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Support programmatic infrastructure.</strong> Touring grants, co-production funding, and multi-year program support can expand performance volume — the second strongest predictor of earned revenue.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-gray">
        <i class="ti ti-building" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Reconsider facility-focused policy.</strong> Space ownership shows no direct association with earned revenue. Policies centered on physical infrastructure may be less effective than those targeting operational capacity.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="finding-item">
      <div class="finding-icon fi-amber">
        <i class="ti ti-refresh" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <p class="finding-text"><strong>Design grants to break the starvation cycle.</strong> Funding structures should enable overhead investment — treating administrative and operational capacity as preconditions for self-reliance, not as waste.</p>
    </li>
  </ul>
  <div class="highlight-box">Policy should function as capacity investment, not revenue substitution — shifting from short-term program outputs toward the organizational conditions that make earned revenue growth possible.</div>
</div>

<style>
.btn-disabled {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 6px;
  font-size: 13px;
  font-weight: 500;
  padding: 7px 16px;
  background: transparent;
  color: #bbb;
  border: 0.5px solid #ddd;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: default;
}

.tag-featured {
  background: #2489b1;
  color: #fff;
  font-size: 11px;
  padding: 2px 8px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  font-weight: 500;
}

.tag-conf {
  background: #daeef6;
  color: #1a6f8a;
  font-size: 11px;
  padding: 2px 8px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  font-weight: 500;
}

.timeline {
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 1.5rem;
}

.timeline::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: 5px;
  top: 8px;
  bottom: 8px;
  width: 1px;
  background: #e5e5e5;
}

.tl-item {
  position: relative;
  padding-bottom: 1.5rem;
}

.tl-item:last-child { padding-bottom: 0; }

.tl-dot {
  position: absolute;
  left: -1.5rem;
  top: 4px;
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  border: 2px solid #4ba8c8;
  background: #fff;
}

.tl-dot.tl-done { background: #4ba8c8; }

.tl-date {
  font-size: 11px;
  color: #999;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}

.tl-title {
  font-size: 14px;
  font-weight: 500;
  color: #1a1a1a;
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}

.tl-desc {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: #666;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
</style>
```
